title: Sociology Vocabulary
description: Key terms: inequality, stratification, institution, socialization, capital (social/cultural).
summary_pt_br: Sociologia: desigualdade, estratificação, instituição, socialização, capital social/cultural.

scenes.subtitle_en: Key terms: inequality, stratification, institution, socialization, capital (social/cultural).
scenes.subtitle_pt: Sociologia: desigualdade, estratificação, instituição, socialização, capital social/cultural.

scenes.text_en: Key terms: inequality, stratification, institution, socialization, capital (social/cultural). Focus: sociology vocabulary. Social capital is often inherited more than it is earned.
scenes.text_pt: Sociologia: desigualdade, estratificação, instituição, socialização, capital social/cultural. Foco: sociology vocabulary. Capital social é mais herdado do que conquistado.

scenes.title: Sociology Vocabulary
scenes.body_en: Core concepts: INEQUALITY, STRATIFICATION, INSTITUTION, SOCIALIZATION, SOCIAL CAPITAL, CULTURAL CAPITAL, STIGMA, MARGINALIZATION.
scenes.body_pt: Sociologia: desigualdade, estratificação, instituição, socialização, capital social/cultural.

examples.en: Social capital is often inherited more than it is earned.
examples.pt: Capital social é mais herdado do que conquistado.

examples.en: Institutions shape behavior long before individuals notice.
examples.pt: Instituições moldam comportamento antes de percebermos.

examples.en: Stigma operates through silence as much as through words.
examples.pt: Estigma opera pelo silêncio tanto quanto pelas palavras.

exercise.question: Complete: "Social capital is often ___ more than it is earned."
exercise.explanation: Full sentence: "Social capital is often inherited more than it is earned."
exercise.hint_pt_br: Capital social é mais herdado do que conquistado.

exercise.question: Which sentence is correct?
options: I haven't heard anything about that.
Institutions shape behavior long before individuals notice.
exercise.explanation: Correct: "Institutions shape behavior long before individuals notice."
exercise.hint_pt_br: Instituições moldam comportamento antes de percebermos.

exercise.explanation: Key terms from the lesson — match each to its meaning.

scenes.title: Sociology Vocabulary — dialog
scenes.pt_summary: Diálogo sobre sociology vocabulary. Pratique as frases marcadas.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Define social capital in one sentence.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: The networks and trust that open doors.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: How is it different from cultural capital?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Cultural capital is taste, knowledge, credentials.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: What's the effect of stigma on opportunity?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: It narrows access even when laws don't.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Sociology is invisible until it's pointed out.

scenes.title: The sociology seminar
scenes.pt_summary: Ouça com atenção. Depois, escreva em inglês o que entendeu — resumo, análise, e sua opinião crítica. O professor avaliará.
scenes.prompt_en: Summarize three concepts discussed. Use at least three sociological terms.
scenes.prompt_pt: Escreva em inglês o que ouviu. Análise crítica com quem falou, argumentos, e sua reflexão.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: In the afternoon seminar, Kumar guided graduate students through the week's readings on inequality and institutions.

paragraphs.speaker: Kumar
paragraphs.en: Social capital is often inherited more than it is earned, and that distinction changes every policy debate.

paragraphs.speaker: Student
paragraphs.en: So cultural capital — credentials, tastes, fluencies — reinforces the same stratification across generations?

paragraphs.speaker: Kumar
paragraphs.en: Exactly. And stigma operates through silence as much as through explicit exclusion in most institutions.

paragraphs.speaker: Student
paragraphs.en: Redesigning institutional defaults might be the single highest-leverage intervention we could possibly make.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: Sociology, Kumar insisted, becomes visible only when someone names the water the fish has always been swimming in.

scenes.title: Recap reading
scenes.passage_en: At Amazing School, the advanced class just finished "Sociology Vocabulary". Key terms: inequality, stratification, institution, socialization, capital (social/cultural). Social capital is often inherited more than it is earned. Institutions shape behavior long before individuals notice. Mr. Kumar reminded the students that at this level, reading widely and writing daily are non-negotiable.

check.question: According to Mr. Kumar, what is non-negotiable at this level?
check.explanation: At C-level, wide reading and daily writing are essential.
check.hint_pt_br: Leitura ampla e escrita diária são essenciais no C.

scenes.title: Listen to Bia
scenes.audio_text_en: Hello! I'm Bia, from Praia do Sol. Today in advanced class we studied "Sociology Vocabulary". It was demanding but genuinely rewarding. See you next session!

scenes.title: Go deeper — advanced resources
scenes.body_pt: Fontes avançadas pra aprofundar. Leitura regular é o que separa um aluno avançado de um proficiente.
